county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Formed 1800 from Allegheny and Lycoming counties. Site of the world's first commercial oil well (Drake Well, 1859, Titusville). Oil City was the center of the early petroleum industry. Named for a Native American term. French and Indian War fort sites.
county_metadata.research_tips: Venango County Historical Society in Franklin has oil industry heritage and genealogical collections. The Drake Well Museum in Titusville documents the birth of the petroleum industry. Recorder of Deeds has records from 1800. For pre-1800 check Allegheny and Lycoming counties. Oil industry records from 1859 document thousands of workers and speculators who came to the Oil Region. The boom-and-bust population makes census records particularly revealing. Cross-reference with Crawford and Forest counties. Presbyterian, Methodist, and Lutheran records serve early families. Oil City and Franklin have distinct industrial community records. The Allegheny River communities have early settlement documentation.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Deed records from 1800; oil lease records significant

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed and mortgage records from 1800. Oil lease records from 1859 era significant.
Wills, administrations from 1800.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage license applications from 1885.
Federal census from 1800. Oil boom population documented in 1860-1870.

source_profiles.source_name: Presbyterian and Methodist Records — Venango Co
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Presbyterian, Methodist, and Lutheran records. Oil boom-era churches.
Oil City Derrick, Franklin papers with oil industry coverage.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Good coverage. Oil industry papers valuable.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Formed 1800 from Allegheny and Lycoming counties. Northwestern PA. Allegheny National Forest partially in county. Named for General Joseph Warren. Oil, timber, and manufacturing industries. Kinzua Dam (1965) displaced Seneca communities.
county_metadata.research_tips: Warren County Historical Society maintains genealogical files and industrial heritage records. Recorder of Deeds has records from 1819 (county organized). For pre-1800 check Allegheny and Lycoming counties. Oil and timber industry records document workers. The Kinzua Dam displacement of Seneca Nation communities is documented in NPS and NARA records. Presbyterian, Methodist, and Lutheran records serve early families. Cross-reference with Forest, McKean, and Erie counties. The Allegheny River was a major transportation route for lumber. Warren's industrial heritage includes manufacturing and oil refining.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed and mortgage records from county organization.
Wills, administrations from 1819.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Formed 1781 from Westmoreland County. Named for George Washington. Southwestern PA. Major agricultural county. Washington and Jefferson College (1781/1802). Whiskey Rebellion center. Bituminous coal mining. Sheep farming heritage. One of the earliest western PA counties.
county_metadata.research_tips: Washington County Historical Society maintains excellent genealogical collections including family files, cemetery records, and Whiskey Rebellion materials. Recorder of Deeds has records from 1781, some of the oldest in western PA. For pre-1781 check Westmoreland County. The Scotch-Irish Presbyterian heritage is exceptionally strong; check early Presbyterian church records (Cross Creek, Upper Buffalo, West Alexander). Washington and Jefferson College archives have local manuscripts. Coal mining in southern townships generated immigrant records. The Whiskey Rebellion (1794) records are at the historical society and PA State Archives. The National Road passed through Washington. Citizens Library has genealogical materials. Cross-reference with Greene and Fayette counties.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Deed records from 1781; online index. Among oldest in western PA

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed and mortgage records from 1781. Online index. Among oldest in western PA.

Federal census from 1790. Whiskey Rebellion era families documented.
Scotch-Irish Presbyterian records from 1775. Cross Creek, Upper Buffalo, West Alexander congregations. Among earliest western PA church records.
Washington newspapers from 1790s. Whiskey Rebellion coverage. Coal industry news.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Formed 1798 from Northampton County. Named for General Anthony Wayne. Northeastern PA. Delaware and Lackawaxen rivers. First commercial railroad in America (Stourbridge Lion, 1829) ran here. Tourism and resort area. Rural agricultural character.
county_metadata.research_tips: Wayne County Historical Society in Honesdale maintains genealogical files and railroad heritage records. Recorder of Deeds has records from 1798. For pre-1798 check Northampton County. The Stourbridge Lion railroad heritage connects to Delaware and Hudson Canal records. Resort and tourism generated employment records. German, Dutch, and English settlers documented in Lutheran, Reformed, and Methodist records. Cross-reference with Pike and Susquehanna counties. The Lackawaxen area has early settlement records. Zane Grey lived in Lackawaxen; his papers at Ohio State but local materials at historical society. Gravity Railroad records document unique transportation system.
county_metadata.heritage_book: History of Wayne, Pike, and Monroe Counties (1886)

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Formed 1773 from Bedford County. One of the earliest western PA counties. Named for Westmoreland, England. Major parent county for western PA. Greensburg is the county seat. Steel, coal, and manufacturing industries. Fort Ligonier (1758). Scotch-Irish and German settlement.
county_metadata.research_tips: Westmoreland County Historical Society in Greensburg has outstanding genealogical collections including family files, church records, and early court records. Recorder of Deeds has records from 1773, critical parent county records for western PA. For pre-1773 check Bedford County. As parent or grandparent county for many western PA counties, Westmoreland records are essential for regional research. Fort Ligonier Association has French and Indian War materials. Scotch-Irish Presbyterian records from 1770s are among the earliest western PA church records. The coal and steel industry in the Latrobe, Jeannette, and New Kensington areas generated immigrant records. Saint Vincent College archives (Benedictine) have Catholic heritage records. Seton Hill University has local history. The Hanna's Town (first county seat) site has archaeological records.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Deed records from 1773; online index. Critical parent county for western PA

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed and mortgage records from 1773. Online index. Parent county records critical.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Formed 1842 from Luzerne County. Named for the Wyoming Valley (not the state). Susquehanna River county. Agricultural with some anthracite mining in southern portion. Tunkhannock is the county seat. New England settlers from Connecticut claims.
county_metadata.research_tips: Wyoming County Historical Society in Tunkhannock maintains genealogical files. Recorder of Deeds has records from 1842. For pre-1842 check Luzerne County. Connecticut settlers under Susquehanna Company documented in published Susquehanna Company Papers. New England heritage means Baptist and Congregational church records. Anthracite mining in southern townships brought immigrant workers. Cross-reference with Luzerne, Lackawanna, and Sullivan counties. The Susquehanna River communities have distinct settlement patterns. Agricultural families documented through deed and census records. Tunkhannock area has the most concentrated records.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Formed 1749 from Lancaster County. One of the earliest PA counties. York served as the US capital 1777-1778 when Continental Congress met here and adopted the Articles of Confederation. Strong German and Scotch-Irish heritage. Industrial county (York Peppermint Patties, Harley-Davidson). Agricultural heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: York County Heritage Trust (formerly Historical Society of York County) is an outstanding genealogical resource with extensive collections including church records, family files, estate inventories, and the York County Archives which houses deeds, wills, and court records in a professional setting. Recorder of Deeds has records from 1749. For pre-1749 check Lancaster County. The German heritage is strong; German Reformed and Lutheran church records are extensive and many have been published. The Scotch-Irish settled the western and southern areas; check Presbyterian records. York County Archives on West Market Street is a model county archival facility. The Continental Congress records from 1777-1778 York session are at Library of Congress and NARA. Harley-Davidson archives document industrial heritage from 1903. Cross-reference with Adams and Cumberland counties.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Deed records from 1749; online index. Colonial-era land records
